votes.reason: The Python code implements the exact same logic as the Coq code, including helper functions for case checking and conversion, and the conditional logic based on whether the string contains letters.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq logic, including character case checking, case conversion, and the main string manipulation logic.

The Python code implements the exact same logic as the Coq code, including the helper functions for case conversion, checking for letters, and the main logic of either swapping cases or reversing the string.

The Python code faithfully implements the same logic as the Coq code: it checks if a character is a lowercase or uppercase letter using the same ASCII range comparisons, converts case by adding/subtracting 32 from the ASCII value, checks if the string contains any letters, and either maps change_case over all characters or reverses the string accordingly. The solve_impl function produces identical results for all concrete string inputs.

The Python code faithfully implements the same logic as the Coq code: it checks if a character is a lowercase or uppercase letter using the same ASCII range comparisons, converts case by adding/subtracting 32 from the ASCII value, checks if the string contains any letters, and either swaps cases of all characters or reverses the string if no letters exist. The pre-condition is trivially true in both, and the spec checks equality with solve_impl's result in both cases.

The Python code faithfully implements the same logic as the Coq code: it checks if a character is a lowercase or uppercase letter using the same ASCII range comparisons, converts case by adding/subtracting 32 from the ASCII value, checks if the string contains any letters, and either maps change_case over all characters or reverses the string accordingly. The solve_impl function produces identical results for all concrete string inputs.
Same logic: detect ASCII letters; if any, toggle case for letters and keep others; otherwise reverse the string. Matches Coq behavior on all inputs.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: detects ASCII letters via range checks, toggles case by ±32 for letters, otherwise leaves characters unchanged, and reverses the string only if it contains no letters. Mapping and reversal behavior align with the Coq spec.
Both detect ASCII letters and toggle their case; if no letters are present, they reverse the string. For all ASCII inputs, results align.
